candidates.stmt: An Eldene resident for over 20 years, I love representing the area as a parish and borough councillor and am keen to continue doing so!  I have improved the infrastructure of the area with new roads, signs and community facilities, and regularly take to the streets with my pressure washer and litter picker to clean signs and tidy up, as well as taking part in community events.  I taught maths at St Joseph's for 14 years, and now work as a Financial Analyst.  As a councillor I am chair of Adults and Health scrutiny committee and Active Travel Champion, and have a strong interest in improving council systems to make them work better for residents.

I am thrilled to have been selected as the Borough council candidate for the Covingham and Dorcan ward (which includes Nythe and North Liden). I have lived in Swindon with my family for the past 30 years and am a mother to three young adults.

I started my working life at WH Smith in Greenbridge before embarking on my academic journey. I am now an academic Criminologist (PhD) working on how best to tackle crime and antisocial behaviour and support victims of crime.

I serve on South Swindon Parish Council, as a parish councillor for Lawn and Badbury Park. I am committed to being a hard working responsive and accessible councillor. I care deeply about my community and I love getting out and about, meeting and talking to residents, listening to their concerns and helping to resolve issues. If I am elected next year I aim to work with residents and the local parish council  to tackle the problems that matter most to local people in Covingham and Dorcan.

I am determined to help build a better Swindon, to engage with and listen to residents, to support communities, to build a safe, cohesive, inclusive and progressive community for all.

Born and raised in Swindon, Marianne has served as a parish councillor for Badbury Park since 2023 and Borough Councillor for Liden, Eldene and Park South since 2024. A psychotherapist and NHS CAMHS lead, Marianne also champions vulnerable young people as vice chair of the council's Corporate Parenting Board. Outside work, she is a keen swimmer, recently swimming 25,000 metres for Prospect Hospice, and is a devoted dog and cat lover.

Born in Swindon and living in Eldene for almost twenty years, I live and breathe our home. I have served in the Wiltshire Constabulary; have a Master’s Degree in Science and I am a Chartered Geographer in the Royal Geographical Society. I work in the engineering consultancy sector and have had the opportunity to work on many local and major national infrastructure projects (transportation, environmental and water) which has equipped me with many skills that I am keen to bring and utilise within the council.

My personal ethos is that I believe everyone should be treated fairly no matter their background and be given opportunities to do the best they can in life. Swindon has an amazing number of cultures but as a town we lack community and integration, I am keen to see this change to bring everyone together and support each other, especially in such challenging times.

As a scientist, I am always keen to find answers to problems and in this case our council tax. I have never been provided with a satisfactory answer with such when questioning those who are making these decisions. As a frustrated taxpayer, I’m keen to get under the bonnet of the council to find out exactly where money is being wasted and where we can make improvement’s. It has saddened me to see a town so full of potential be neglected and allowed to be run down. It is scandalous that we are not completing with the likes of Bristol, Oxford and Reading. It’s time that we had openness and transparency so that truthful dialogue can take place to forge a path forward.

I am standing for election because I genuinely care about our community, town and country and believe I have the maturity, skills and passion to breathe new life into our troubled town. I get great enjoyment out of helping others but I have never been afraid to stand up for what is right even if that means going against the grain, as a logical thinker I base decisions on fact and not to appease. I am always open to different ideas and sugges

I am proud to be standing as the Conservative candidate to represent Blunsdon, Highworth and our local villages following the retirement of popular Councillor Alan Bishop.

Having served as a Highworth Town Councillor for 8 years, Highworth Mayor in 201 7, a school governor and various local charity and community roles, I have shown I have the experience to make a difference.

As a family we have been heavily involved with supporting the Highworth market, volunteering at the annual May Day and Christmas Lights events, various groups for young people, community litter pics and The Link Magazine. I am a strong voice against inappropriate development and I am an active member of the 'Save The Golf Course Group.'

I also understand the importance of protecting the character of our wonderful villages: Blunsdon, Castle Eaton, Hampton, Hannington, Hannington Wick, Inglesham, Sevenhampton & Stanton Fitzwarren. I will also work with the respective local Town & Parish Councils, press for the final areas to get superfast fibre broadband, support the amazing Blunsdon Community Shop, the Highworth High Street and all the local village pubs.

I would be very proud to represent our community alongside Justin Tomlinson MP, Cllrs Vijay Manro & Steve Weisinger - a strong team. I very much hope you will use your postal vote to vote for me - I will be a hardworking, visible and approachable Councillor.

Rural communities are too often overlooked and ignored when decisions are made. For the past 13 years, I’ve worked on the railway, and alongside that I served for 12 years as a Special Constable in the police. Like many residents, I want to see sensible planning that protects our green spaces, and a council that listens to all residents, including those in villages and rural areas.

Liz is a Scot by birth and moved to the Swindon area in 1988. She is married with two grown-up children and 4 grandsons. Liz worked as a GP in Swindon for 20 years and finished her career in a South West regional health service management role.

Volunteering at Citizens’ Advice, Liz comes into contact with people every week who are struggling as a result of heartless Tory policies.  Years of Tory cuts to council budgets have led to cuts in services and a lack of investment in people. Many local people find that they are unable to get through on the phone to report a problem. Online is touted as a solution, but in practice, the systems are far from user-friendly, and not all of our residents can access them. The system seems designed to save money by limiting people’s access to essential services to which they are entitled.

In healthcare, Boris Johnson’s 2019 manifesto promised that 40 new hospitals would be built by 2030; five years later, there is no sign of them – not even the Hospital 2.0 blueprint, which is supposed to be used, has been finalised. Patients, families and staff are struggling to cope in premises which are dangerous, crumbling and unfit for modern healthcare, while NHS capital budgets are plundered to plug holes in day-to-day funding. The LibDems are calling on the government to release the promised capital so that work can begin on the desperately needed improvements and on immediately replacing the seven hospitals most affected by the RAAC's crumbling concrete.

The Lib Dems have coherent, fair policies developed through their evidence-based, democratic processes to address the fundamental problems faced by the British people after so many years of Tory mismanagement. This is why Liz is a member and wants to represent her local community and become part of the solution.

Local business leader and former Royal Navy officer. I have built and scaled high-growth training organisations, delivering skills, jobs and opportunity which I hope to bring to the community. As a Reform UK candidate I will bring disciplined leadership, economic focus and practical solutions to serve local communities.

I’ve proudly lived in Swindon for 9 years after moving from West London. I work hard and love helping people. My priorities: Safe streets, reduced crime. Cutting waste to help ease the cost of living. More job and training opportunities. I will listen, act, and treat everyone with fairness and respect, regardless of background, culture or race, together we can build a better Swindon.

Growing up in the Ridgeway and Chiseldon ward, I have developed a vast sense of pride for my local area and the community within it. Being involved within the local community, I understand just how much it means to the people of Ridgeway and Chiseldon. I want to be a passionate voice who will advocate for the community's interests, ensuring that local people are heard and that residents feel represented by their councillor. For me, being a councillor is an opportunity to give back to the community, by supporting the needs of residents.

As someone who is young, I can bring a fresh perspective, whilst being pragmatic in my approach. I want to bring a modern mindset and develop new ideas and solutions. Working in education, I understand the gravitas it holds; I want to ensure that schools in Swindon can reach their full potential so that students can thrive and succeed. Also, I want to make sure that Swindon is accessible to everyone, even those in rural areas. I want to bolster transport connections by improving footpaths and bus services. I am someone who is extremely enthusiastic about the ideas they present and is passionate about the cause they push, that's why I want to become a councillor.

I have lived and worked in Swindon all my life. My father served as a town councillor after a career in the military, my grandparents worked for GWR before running a local pub, experiences that grounded me in the values of community & service. I bring a creative, compassionate & community focused approach, with strong skills in research, planning & problem solving.

I have lived in Swindon my whole life. I’m a parent of three children two with SEND and I was diagnosed with dyslexia later in life, giving me insight into the importance of early support & local services. I have over 15 years’ experience leading transformation programmes & working knowledge of Swindon Council. I am focused on building a secure future for our children and our town.

I grew up locally and came back in 2023, shocked by the decline. I’m standing to bring back pride, improve our town and build a Swindon that embraces its potential and offers real opportunity for everyone, not just be a commuter town with a famous roundabout.

I am a dedicated member of the local community, committed to making a positive difference for all of our residents. I understand the real challenges people face every day. Passionate about improving local services, supporting families, and creating opportunities for everyone. I am focused on delivering real solutions that benefit our whole community. I believe in listening, working hard, and standing up for what matters most to residents. Family, Community, Country.

Stanka Wilson has called the Haydon Wick ward home for ten years and has served as a Borough Councillor since 2023. She is committed to building a better community and works tirelessly alongside residents to create lasting improvements. Outside her professional duties, Stanka listens, acts and champions positive change for all of us.

Ravi Venkatesh has been a Borough Councillor since 2023. He has deep roots in the community, with both of his children having attended Haydonleigh School. He is passionate about listening to residents and building a safer, stronger and more connected community for everyone.

Leroy Roberts is a British Army veteran and currently works as the Executive Officer of a Credit Union. As a Parish Councillor and governor of a local junior school, he is hoping to use his community experience to serve the residents of Haydon Wick on Swindon Borough Council.

I love Highworth! Here are my 5 pledges

1/ Invest in nature
I will campaign to improve footpaths, invest in cycle routes  and transform half of the old Highworth Golf Course into a nature reserve,  so we can all enjoy our countryside.

2/ Sort out the roads
Labour has a 4-year plan to fix our roads including the Fox Roundabout, Swindon Road, Grove Hill and Park Avenue.

3/ Raise the standard on council homes
We are investing £250 million in Swindon's council homes to tackle the legacy of damp, mould, and poor conditions.

4/ Be serious about services
Labour is transforming council services for the better while getting costs back under control after years of Tory chaos. I will work with our health and education leaders to ensure services remain fit for the future.

5/ Make green choices
Labour is switching to greener bio fuels, cutting the CO2 emissions of its 675 bin lorries and other vehicles by up to 90 per cent. I will continue to help residents get solar panels to cut energy bills.

I’m Rosie Leather, one of your Green Party candidates for the new Kingshill ward.

Born and raised in Kingshill ward, I am now raising my own young family here while actively supporting local community projects.

I have worked in the public sector for over 20 years, including in the NHS and now in research and innovation. You may recognise me from working at Swindon Pulse Wholefood Co-operative during this time too.

I am a former South Swindon Parish Councillor for the central Swindon area and supported improvements to Westcott Rec, Cambria Bridge and GWR Park.

I am committed to fairness and equality and determined to build a brighter future for Kingshill and Swindon.

Marcus Kittridge is one of your Green Party candidates for the brand new Kingshill Ward.

Marcus has lived in the ward for 45 years, raised his family here and his grandson is growing up in the same community. He wants him, and every young person to inherit the best version of Kingshill and Swindon.

Marcus is standing for election because he wants our community to get the best possible outcomes from the local council; better amenities, practical solutions to long-standing problems, and a stronger voice for residents who deserve to be heard.

Marcus was an engineer for 30 years. He switched career and drove Swindon Dial A Ride community transport minibuses for 8 years and more recently he ran a successful local independent business in the heart of Swindon for the last 10 years.

If elected in the local elections in May this year, Marcus will work tirelessly on your behalf.

Peter Major - Reform UK candidate for Kingshill

Swindon has been my home for 26 years. My family is here, my children grew up here, and my grandchildren are growing up here now. When I talk about safer streets and services that actually work, it's not campaign language - it's a question about the town my grandchildren will inherit.

26 years here is long enough to see how Swindon has changed - both ways. Antisocial behaviour has crept up. Council tax goes up every year while services don't. Potholes outlast the seasons. The town centre after 8pm is not somewhere most residents want to be.

My career has been in IT and finance, working with banks and technology companies in international markets. That means specific skills: reading budgets properly, spotting inefficiency, managing large contracts, and holding suppliers to account. These are standards Swindon Borough Council needs at least as much as any commercial business does.

My priorities as a councillor for Kingshill:

- Safer streets - real action on antisocial behaviour and visible patrols in the town centre, not just reports about plans for reports.
- Council finances - an honest audit of where residents' money actually goes. If the answer isn't clear, that itself is the problem.
- Roads and basic services - a pothole shouldn't survive a full year.
- A real voice for residents in Civic Offices, not just for Labour group insiders.

Reform UK is the only party today willing to cut through bureaucracy and demand answers from those spending your money. I'm asking for your vote on 7 May to bring those standards into the Council Chamber.

Josh is a resident of Lower Stratton where he lives with his family. He wants to play his part in making the area safer and an even better place to live by representing Lower Stratton and Coleview as a borough councillor.

Working for a well know telecomms company where he also serves as a CWU trade union representative, he knows the importance of standing up for working people, listening to concerns and getting things sorted rather than ignored.

He was elected to Stratton Parish Council in 2025 and since then has helped residents with issues and queries and regularly takes part in litter picks and street cleans, tackling things like graffiti and weeds, he now wants to bring that practical, hands-on approach to the Borough Council.

During his spare time Josh is a volunteer with a club supporting adults with disabilities and learning difficulties, including many members from Stratton and Coleview.

Leon has lived in Grange Park for nearly 25 years and in Swindon for over 30 years. Over the past few years as a parish and borough councillor, he's worked hard to deliver for the area - including securing the resurfacing of Hay Land in Grange Park. He's also enjoyed working alongside fantastic local volunteers on projects like rewilding in Toothill Park and village centre litter picks throughout the year, not just at election time.

Leon is a product development manager at a local pharmaceutical development and manufacturing company where he has been employed for over 25 years.  In this role he has gained experience of risk management, negotiation, planning and budgeting all of which help in the role of councillor.

Leon is a keen advocate for environmental concerns and is proud to be an investor in Westmill Wind Farm which is the South of England’s first 100% community owned onshore windfarm   He enjoys running and travel and often combining the two, having ran half Marathons and Marathons in several European cities.

Swindon is my hometown, where I've lived all my life, and I hold a deep sense of community and local pride. With extensive experience, in both public and private sectors, I am committed to using my skills to strengthen the voices of residents in Lydiard, Freshbrook and Toothill, ensuring their concerns are heard and addressed. I am passionate about delivering solutions together and improving critical public services. I believe this work is urgently needed and I am dedicated to making it happen to help our community thrive.

In 2014 the Croft Sports Centre was about to be passed to GLL in a property deal based on the Council’s transfer of the Oasis to Moirai.   As a retired Old Town resident I campaigned to stop this happening and that led to my being elected in 2016 and joining Nadine Watts as a Labour Councillor for Old Town and East Wichel.

I am also Labour Group spokesperson for sustainability.  In both capacities I have had to learn on the job and it has been hard work but very rewarding!  As a Parish Councillor I have been involved with South Swindon Parish’s projects to improve our parks, play areas, allotments and our Library.  I am working for better management of Lawn Woods and the Great Copse, and if re-elected will step up the fight for more forward-looking policies to reduce carbon emissions and increase biodiversity across the Borough.

As an Old Town resident, I’m proud to be standing for election where I Iive.

I have had the honour of being Leader of the Council since 2023, and we have worked hard to rebuild the relationship between the Council and residents.

We have delivered the funding for a new Entertainments Venue, the first new Youth Centre in the Borough in a generation, sorted installed Bus Boulevard and Museum projects, got our Adults services rated as Good and have exciting plans for devolution with the Thames Valley, the ton centre and new jobs. I’m really proud of the emerging Drone sector we are creating the jobs and opportunities it will deliver for Swindon.

Please vote for me as your Labour & Co-operative Party Candidate on May 7th.

As an IT Consultant, Andy has worked for some of the biggest global names in finance, retail, and manufacturing. He moved to Swindon twenty years ago, and currently lives with Bunny, a rescue he met while volunteering for SN Dogs. As chair of the Hreod Burna Urban Forest charity, Andy cares passionately about the natural environment. He believes that we have a duty of care for the world around us, and that people benefit enormously from having easy access to green spaces. But it wasn't the Green Party's environmental credentials that attracted him. He joined the Greens because they have always been committed to the real change needed to deliver the fairer, more just, society that he is striving for. Andy grew up on a council estate, the first in his family to go to university. He will fight for more council houses, and better local schools, to give others the same opportunities that he had: A secure start in life, so that everyone can reach their true potential and lead rich and fulfilling lives. He believes that those with the broadest shoulders should bear more of the financial burden, so we can invest in our NHS to shorten waiting lists and give everyone easier access to local doctors and dentists.

Jocelina has dedicated herself to helping others through her work with the Helping Heart charity. She is driven by compassion and a commitment to making life better for locaal people. Jocelina understands the challenges families face and will fight for fairness and dignity for all. With deep roots in our community, she will be a powerful voice for local people.

Swindon has been my home for 50 years, growing up as a farmer’s daughter and granddaughter of a railway worker, both who also fought for our country, gave me a strong foundation. My personal experiences and challenges in life have given me my own strength, compassion, and the courage to stand up when times get hard. I want to be a voice for the people of Parks & Swindon to listen & act!

I have lived in the Swindon Parks Ward for over 50 years, and I am concerned about the great many potholes and the high cost of house prices and rents. The cost of living crisis has made life very hard for everyone in Swindon and especially the young generation who are just starting out in life.

Jamal has been a Borough Councillor for Walcot and Park North since 2021 and has served on South Swindon Parish Council since it was created. Jamal is passionate about staying in touch with residents, knocking on doors every week throughout the year as well as monthly surgeries at The Shop in Cavendish Square.

Charli has lived in Swindon for her whole life and works in the adult social care sector as a Care Manager. Charli has worked in the sector since 2019 and is passionate about enhancing the lives of those with complex needs.

Charli grew up in North Swindon, before moving to South Swindon in 2024.

Charli cares about the environment and is keen to get locals involved with community efforts to uphold this.

Charli volunteers at an animal sanctuary and a community interest group that runs accessible events for adults with additional needs.

Charli is a member of the Young Greens and wants to do more to engage the younger generation within the local community.

I’m Paul Park, and I’m standing to be your **Labour & Co-operative** councillor because I believe in the power of communities working together to create real change.

I’ve lived in Swindon all my life. When I was younger, I've worked in industries across the town, from the industrial  roles on the Groundwell Industrial Estate all the way to management roles in the corporate world, and people across our community always treated me as one of their own. That’s something I’ve never forgotten and it’s why I feel a strong sense of responsibility to give back.

Growing up at a time when neurodiversity wasn’t widely understood has shaped who I am. It’s why I care deeply about fairness, strong public services, and making sure no one is left behind.

I’m proud to stand as a Labour & Co-operative candidate, because I believe in social justice, equality, solidarity, and the power of people working together to improve our community.

Already delivering locally
- Helped transform an abandoned bowls club into a thriving youth hub for local children
- Set up a code club to support digital skills and opportunities for young people
- Campaigned for practical community safety measures, including a knife bin at council offices
- Pushed for stronger local engagement and more active support for community initiatives

My pledges to you
1. Fix roads and pavements across Priory Vale
Working with the council to ensure roads and footpaths are properly maintained

2. Improve bus services and local transport connections
Pushing for more reliable timetables, better routes, and stronger links across North Swindon

3. Protect green spaces and the River Ray
Working with residents and local groups to protect natural areas and improve access

4. Tackle fly-tipping and keep our neighbourhoods clean
Supporting enforcement action and practical solutions to keep our area tidy and safe

5. Increase youth provision and activities for young people
Building on the success of local youth initiatives to give y

I live in Swindon and have been a local councillor for Eastcott since 2018. I am standing again for re-election in May as a Labour candidate as this is the only party I believe stands up to issues of equal rights and opportunities, too many people are being left behind under the current system and local administration.

Swindon residents are crying out for change and this can only be achieved by a change of local Government.

I am standing to be re-elected this May for Labour because I have met so many people who complain that they are not happy with the decisions made by the local Tory administration. Increases in Council Tax, bad roads and lack of any community support.

I have met families who are  barely able to afford to feed their kids, I have met   teachers leaving the profession because of education cuts and young people unable to get anywhere decent to live. Cuts to funding in Police have increased crime.

I want to help reverse the inequaliites that have been perpetuated under this  Tory administration, which I believe are so detrimental to the whole of Swindon rich and poor alike.

Residents like the policies Labour want to introduce. We want more police on the streets, investment in local communities, Health and Education and a halt to the decline in the infrastructure of our town. I believe this can be achieved if our priorities change but it will only happen if you vote Labour. We have to reverse the gross inequalities that force families on low incomes to rely on food banks and we have to pursue policies that promote genuine social inclusion.

I am actively involved in the community where I live in successfully bringing communities together, by hosting local events, raising money for local charities and working co-operatively with others in promoting equal rights, opportunities and responsibilities.  During the pandemic I have worked with Public Health England to make local community videos, led a food bank from my garage.

If re-elected I will use th

Paul has been a councillor in Swindon representing Eastcott ward since 2015. He is currently the Cabinet Member for Children's Social Care. As well as being a diligent and knowledgeable ward councillor, Paul has also worked hard on improving services for children and families, building a fairer Swindon for everyone.

I’m an Engineer, lecturer, and teacher passionate about education and the environment. 
 I studied Environmentally sensitive design and Energy infrastructure so I understand why Swindon council in trying to adopt Ed Milliband's NetZero policy is not only damaging to local industry but also causing more long term damage to the environment and our energy indipendence by outsourcing our manufacture abroad.

As a Christian, I believe in truth and supporting those that feel overlooked. I’m standing to strengthen our community, support our youth, and ensure better outcomes for local families and future generations. 
 If elected I'm keen to work in Adult or Childrens social services, which need improvement. As an educator, I can work with the leadership to reform these services.
Let’s build a better Swindon!

I’m a family man who wants a safe, thriving area for my family. I’ve supported the community by editing and designing the RCRA newsletter for 7 years and creating materials for The Kelly Foundation. I’m standing for change to reduce waste and help improve our local area.

I've lived in Westlea since moving to Swindon in 1988 to begin my nurse training at Princess Margaret Hospital. I've served as a councillor for twenty years. I've loved raising my children here, and now my grandchildren. Like you, I want the best for our community.

Rose grew up in Swindon and is now bringing up her young family in Wroughton. She is a qualified accountant who wants to see improved services and good value for money for her neighbours in West Swindon. She serves as Vice Chair of West Swindon Parish Council and is committed to working hard for and being available to local residents.

I am a research scientist and engineer who returned from the United States and became active in localcampaigns to support my community. I am standing to be your borough councillor to grow the local economy, create skilled jobs, and champion science in local politics.

Matt Barnes is a commercial gas engineer and community football coach with a strong commitment to fairness, sustainability, and local action.\n\nHe is standing for the Green Party to push for real change—tackling the cost of living crisis, improving public services, and protecting the environment for future generations
Through his work and coaching, Matt sees first hand the importance of strong communities and opportunities for young people. As someone who follows a vegan lifestyle, he believes in making positive, practical choices that support a more sustainable and compassionate future.
Matt is committed to listening to residents and being a strong, honest voice for the community.

Delighted to be able to announce that I have been unanimously reselected to contest the new South Marston Ward in the May 2026 all-out elections.

Boundary changes mean the current Stratton St Margaret and South Marston Ward gets split into a 3 councillor Lower Stratton Ward and a 1 councillor South Marston Ward.

Over the last three years, I’ve worked consistently to tackle the local issues, whether it is flooding, development issues, construction vehicles, stopping the traveller camp, and car break-ins; supported community action days like tree planting and litter picks; and other events across the village - working in partnership with South Marston Parish Council.

I hope I get the chance to continue for four more years next May and I hope I can count on your support!

I moved to Swindon in 1987 and I have lived here happily with my family since then.

I enjoyed a successful career as an ‘Economics and Business Studies’ teacher, Head of Sixth Form and latterly Secondary Deputy Headteacher of a large Secondary School. My first job was at St Joseph’s School on Nythe Road.

In recent years I’ve become more involved in volunteering and community work. I’m a member of the North Swindon Leisure and Libraries Trust.

I’ve been out and about with Councillor Tom Butcher, in Stratton St Margret and South Marsden, we have been litter picking and tree planting. We have also been talking to hundreds of people about what they want and issues that need raising with the council.

Chris is new to Swindon and lives in St Andrews.

He works as a video game developer, most notably on the family-friendly LEGO games, has founded two businesses, and often speaks about careers in that industry at schools and universities.

Chris is a keen advocate of energy-efficient homes and renewable technology.

He is standing as a Green Party candidate because they offer a manifesto of democratic, evidence-based policies to create a future we can all enjoy.

I’ve lived in Swindon for most of my life and want to make the future brighter for younger people and all residents. I’m a retired local, not a career politician, standing for Reform UK to cut waste, improve roads, support families, and restore pride in our town under the values of Family, Community, Country.

I’ve lived in St Andrews Ward since 1999 with my family and grandchildren nearby. After 32 years as an RAF aircraft engineer, plus MOD and BAE roles, I retired to focus on local service. As a parish councillor since 2017, I stand for Reform UK to bring fresh, common-sense leadership and real change to our community.

Better Streets. Better Services.
I am standing as your Green candidate for St Andrews & Tadpole to bring real accountability to local decisions.
We need: 
More GPs, schools & local services
Safer roads & better planning
Spaces for young people
Transparent budgets - your money, your say
Vote Green on 7th May 2026
Let’s put our community first.
#VoteGreen #Swindon

I’m proud to have been born and raised in Walcot, where my family were among the first to settle in 1950s. Growing up, our community was vibrant—full of local clubs, a busy library, and places where families came together. As I approach retirement, I want to give back to the area that shaped me and restore pride, community spirit, and a sense of belonging in Walcot & wider to all of Swindon.

I have lived in Swindon for more than 22 years, I have contributed in my own small way, by volunteering at GW Hospital, supporting the local temple & charity food run services. These experiences strengthened my belief in compassion, fairness & community responsibility. My focus is on strengthening local services, supporting families & helping build a connected community.

Dear Fellow Local Resident,

It has been a privilege to repesent you since 2016 and I am asking you to re-elect me as your Conservative Councillor on Thursday 6th May.

I was first elected on the pledge to protect Lydiard Park and keep it in public hands.  I delivered on that pledge, and working with my Conservative colleagues, we have seen more investment made into our Park, including my recent success in securing £28,000 for play park refurbishment.

I also kept my promise to make the Hay Lane crossing safer, with double yellow lines and the introduction of a School Safety Zone.

I have worked to help make West Swindon cleaner and greener, with regular litter picks and sponsoring the Parish wildflower and bulb planting schemes, plus extending hours at the West Swindon Library with the support of our Conservative Parish Council.

As the Council's Armed Forces Champion, I have been supporting our armed forces and veteran families with housing issues and gaining access to council services.

My pledges to you for my next term are to secure even more investment in Lydiard Park, such as better car parks and toilets, and to work with the Council to see more enforcement action on fly tipping and littering.  I will also work to get more of our local roads resurfaced and will campaign for accessible playgrounds for children of all abilities.

Please vote for me so that I can carry on delivering on the promises I make to you.

Your sincerely,

Matthew Courtliff

I’m Martin Barrett, I grew up and was educated in Swindon. I joined the navy and came to Wroughton in 1989 with my family, my wife Melanie and four children. We have always been involved in the village, supporting the scouts and football club. I have always been a keen supporter of the locals with sport. I would like to help the village supporting the young people as well as all residents in having sporting facilities, that unfortunately we still don’t have.

I was a founder member of Wroughton junior football club and started Wroughton twinning with our our twin town, Saint-Germain in France. Children of all ages took part in a football tournament organised by our host St Germain St Pierre. I am a member of the North Wiltshire Woodturners and tutor at the W.B. Men’s Shed.

I am supporting the Wichelstowe Health Centre as we need this as quickly as possible. The demand on the Wroughton Health Centre has been so much that we need the new health centre for all the people in Wroughton and Wichelstowe.

There are so many potholes in the village and the lack of repair, it is not just the roads, it is the pavements which are a blight, as a lot of young children using the schools and for the elderly to get around the village, they need to be sorted as soon as possible.

I’m Stan Pajak, I was born in Swindon, and I’ve lived here here all of my life. I am married to Dawn and we have four children. My two daughters perhaps following their mum are involved in education in local schools with one a secondary school assistant head and the other teaches at primary level. As for my two sons one works in Epic Games as a QA specialist - just as well he ignored me telling him not to waste his time playing games! While the other is a project manager in digital media. We have four grandchildren. Which I’m told keep us young!

It was very early on in my life I realised I wanted to get involved in local things and local politics. At work I became a trade union shop steward working for people and helped fight the threat of redundancies.

I first got elected in 1985 as a Wiltshire County Councillor when the Liberals together with the SDP took control. In 2002 I became mayor of Swindon. It was an amazing year. I became one of the few mayors ever to take part in the Moonwalk a walking marathon through the night time streets of London raising funds for breast cancer. I had the honour of speaking for the Council at the funeral of Wroughton’s famed councillor Harry Garrett who is remembered in the village through Harry Garrett Court.

Having retired I work as a volunteer at the Prospect as a volunteer at their Media shop. I have never been a great athlete but enjoy running and last year ran the Swindon Half Marathon raising funds for the Prospect. My marathon running days are over!

This is my second time standing for Wroughton and Wichelstowe and the issues have not changed under the Conservative and Labour administrations - too many houses in Wroughton under both the local plans of the Tories and Labour, our campaign for a new Health Centre in Wichelstowe - it should have been built years ago and the tackling of the disgraceful amount of potholes on our local roads.

If elected I will be dedicated to the people of Wroughton and Wichelstowe keeping in t

My name is Elmira, and I have lived in Old Town, Swindon, since 2011. I moved here and built my life here, and this community has become my home. I am a single mum of three young children, all attending local schools, so like many parents, I experience first-hand the realities of family life in Swindon.

I have watched parts of Wichelstowe grow from open fields into a thriving neighbourhood. I remember when the first buildings went up and when The Deanery School opened its doors. I have seen how decisions made locally shape our community -  for better or worse -  and I believe those decisions must always put families first.

I hold a higher degree in Psychology and Human Resources, and I work as a Technical Recruiter in a technology company. My professional background has taught me the importance of listening carefully, understanding people’s needs, and making balanced, evidence-based decisions. Every day, I work with individuals from different backgrounds and countries, which strengthens my belief in international collaboration and cooperation.

As a single parent, I understand the pressures many families face — especially with the rising cost of living. I know how difficult it can be to balance work, childcare, and financial responsibility. That is why I care deeply about practical support for families, accessible education, and strong local services.
